sections.instruction: Questions 1-5: Circle the appropriate letters A-D. Questions 6 and 7: Complete Dad’s note. Questions 8-10: Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS to complete each space.

questions.content: How long did Hannah think it would take her to find a place to live?
There is not enough accommodation to rent because
options: A it is the end of the academic year
B Hannah is a new student
C the area has lots of new technology companies

questions.content: £400 a month for rent is
options: A higher than Hannah has paid before
B lower than Hannah has paid before
C not cheap for the area

questions.content: At the moment Hannah is living
Hannah’s new flat
options: B is on the second floor
D has a large roof terrace

questions.content: Complete Dad’s note. Hannah’s address: (6) {{BLANK}} Whitehart Road (7) {{BLANK}} 9RJ.
Hannah plans to travel to her parents’ house on Friday {{BLANK}}
Hannah’s Dad will return the van on {{BLANK}}
The journey time is about {{BLANK}}

sections.instruction: Questions 11-14: Circle FOUR letters A-H. Questions 15-18: Tick Column A if the individual is in favour of the proposals. Tick Column B if the individual is against the proposals. Questions 19 and 20: Circle the correct letter A-D.

questions.content: Which FOUR planned developments are mentioned?
options: C a play area for children

questions.content: Tick Column A if the individual is in favour of the proposals. Tick Column B if the individual is against the proposals.

D a town with a university

questions.content: The College has
options: A never had a 100% success rate
B had a 100% success rate this year

sections.instruction: Questions 21-25: Write NO MORE THAN FOUR WORDS or A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 26-30: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

sections.instruction: Questions 31-35: Circle the correct letters A-D. Questions 36-39: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer. Question 40: Choose the correct letter A-D.
sections.passage_or_context: Employees now working (36) {{BLANK}} physically and mentally. Productivity up in many (37) {{BLANK}}. In 2002, local car plant vehicle per employee on rise to (38) {{BLANK}}. Car industry once thought to be (39) {{BLANK}}.

options: A Most workers say they do not take all their holidays.
B Under half of the workers say they do not take all their holidays.
C Employers do not believe workers.
D About a third of healthy workers take days off sick.
